name: network-protocol-skills
description: Codex skills for choosing, planning, implementing, and diagnosing modern application transports and real-time networking protocols, including QUIC, HTTP/3, WebRTC, Media over QUIC, WebTransport-adjacent handoffs, protocol maturity checks, and stack-specific implementation routing.

interface.shortDescription: Modern transport, real-time protocol, and diagnostics workflows for Codex.
interface.longDescription: Guide Codex agents through modern application transport and real-time networking decisions with protocol maturity checks, QUIC and HTTP/3 planning, Media over QUIC draft-aware workflow guidance, WebRTC signaling and media/data-channel routing, WebTransport-adjacent handoffs, network diagnostics, deployment constraints, and implementation handoffs into the owning language, web, mobile, server, and cloud plugins.
defaultPrompt: Choose the right network transport for this feature before implementation.
Plan an HTTP/3 or QUIC change and identify the stack-specific implementation handoff.
Assess whether Media over QUIC fits this real-time media workflow and what draft state must be checked.
Plan or diagnose a WebRTC feature, including signaling, ICE, DTLS, SRTP, data channels, TURN, and browser/runtime constraints.
Diagnose this protocol, proxy, CDN, browser, UDP, TLS, ALPN, Alt-Svc, or NAT traversal failure with concrete evidence.
